subhead.line1: Deliver micro-lessons directly where your teams already work.

email.description: Track engagement, progress, and impact across teams with clear reporting.

benefits.3: Clear analytics and reporting for HR.

form.success: Thanks - your message was sent successfully.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.title: Choose the setup that matches your growth stage
hero.subhead: This page is built as a guided pricing explorer instead of a simple pricing table, so visitors can understand what changes from one plan to the next.

explorer.title: Start with the stage you are in
explorer.subhead: Pick the option that feels closest to your current team. You can replace all of this placeholder text later.

starter.name: Starter
starter.tagline: For small teams testing their first assistant flows.
starter.summary: A lighter entry point with the essentials you need to launch, validate, and learn what your users actually need.
starter.idealFor: Teams that want a simple pilot with a small number of users and a very clear scope.
highlights: Cover a small team or pilot group

growth.name: Growth
growth.tagline: For companies turning one use case into a real internal product.
growth.summary: This is the main plan for teams that already see value and now want better automation, broader access, and more room to grow.
growth.idealFor: Teams that want a stronger operational setup and expect more users, messages, and workflows.
highlights: Expand to more assistants and channels
Support more teams with less manual work

scale.name: Scale
scale.tagline: For larger rollouts, advanced governance, and tailored setups.
scale.summary: Built for companies that need broader control, more integrations, and pricing adapted to their structure and volume.
scale.idealFor: Organizations with multiple teams, more stakeholders, and a need for custom limits or rollout planning.

billingInfo.title: Simple placeholder billing logic
billingInfo.text: For now this is fake content only. Later you can replace it with exact billing rules, annual discounts, usage details, and add-on pricing.

setup.title: Setup and rollout

automation.title: Automation and AI
automation.description: Use this area to show what becomes more powerful as plans grow.

governance.title: Governance and control
governance.description: This is useful later when you add permissions, audit needs, or enterprise requirements.

items.question: Can I change plans later?
items.answer: Yes. This is placeholder text, but this area is perfect for upgrade, downgrade, and migration explanations.

items.question: Are there setup fees?
items.answer: You can later explain setup fees, onboarding packages, or implementation costs here.

items.question: Do you offer annual billing?
items.answer: Yes. This section can later describe annual discounts, invoice terms, and contract logic.

items.question: What happens if I need something custom?
items.answer: Use this answer to explain enterprise requests, custom integrations, or special rollout needs.

finalCta.title: Let’s find the right plan together
finalCta.text: Keep this as your closing section with one direct CTA and one lower-friction secondary option.

Templates.name: Name

Assistants.placeholder: Select an assistant from the left column.

CreateAssistant.title: Create new Assistant
CreateAssistant.name: Name
CreateAssistant.nameHelp: Name that will be associated with the Assistant.
CreateAssistant.descriptionHelp: A brief description about the Assistant.
CreateAssistant.modelHelp: AI model the Assistant will use.
CreateAssistant.creativityHelp: Controls diversity of answers: at 0.5, the Assistant picks among higher-probability options, keeping to the most relevant ones.
CreateAssistant.varietyHelp: Controls randomness: lower values lead to more predictable replies. Closer to zero becomes more deterministic.

Reports.title: See adoption, activity, and assistant usage in one place
Reports.subtitle: This first version is grounded in the data the app already stores today: users, assistants, threads, channels, and messages.
Reports.noOrg: We could not find an organization for this account yet.

sections.activitySubtitle: The last 14 days help you spot momentum changes quickly without opening individual user threads.
sections.channelsSubtitle: Use this to see whether adoption is happening more through WhatsApp or Teams.
sections.assistantsSubtitle: A quick read on which assistants are actually assigned, active, and carrying conversation volume.
sections.ideasSubtitle: These fit the product direction well, but some of them need extra event tracking before the numbers become trustworthy.

users.title: New users per day
users.subtitle: Last 14 days
users.empty: No new users in this period.

noIssues.title: No obvious blockers in this snapshot
noIssues.text: Nothing urgent stands out from these baseline metrics right now.

unassigned.title: Unassigned users
unassigned.text: Users exist without an assistant attached, which can break onboarding or automation consistency.

quiet.title: Users with no activity
quiet.text: These users have no thread history yet, so they have not actually engaged with the product.

idleAssistants.title: Idle assistants
idleAssistants.text: Some assistants exist but still have no message traffic. This usually means rollout is incomplete or discoverability is weak.

roadmap.title: Broadcast performance
roadmap.label: Needs campaign events
roadmap.text: Delivery, template usage, reply rate, and opt-out rate by message or campaign.

roadmap.title: Learning outcomes
roadmap.label: Needs lesson events
roadmap.text: Completion, quiz scores, and progression by assistant, team, or user segment.

roadmap.title: Retention cohorts
roadmap.label: Needs return-visit logic
roadmap.text: How many users who start in week 1 are still active in weeks 2, 4, and 8.

roadmap.title: AI quality signals
roadmap.label: Needs outcome tracking
roadmap.text: Fallback rate, unanswered intents, handoff moments, and response quality over time.
